title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Haemul Pajeon Recipe | Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Haemul Pajeon, a crispy Korean seafood pancake, with our easy recipe. Perfect for a delicious appetizer or meal at home!
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake) Recipe
h2Headings: How to Make Haemul Pajeon: Step-by-Step Guide
Pro Tips for the Crispiest Haemul Pajeon
Frequently Asked Questions About Haemul Pajeon
seo.imageAlt: Crispy Haemul Pajeon, an authentic Korean seafood pancake with shrimp, squid, and green onions, served with a savory dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Master the art of making Haemul Pajeon, a beloved Korean seafood pancake, perfect for any occasion.

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake loaded with fresh seafood and crisp green onions, pan-fried to a golden perfection. Its crispy edges and tender interior make it an irresistible appetizer or a light meal. Served with a tangy soy-based dipping sauce, this dish offers a delightful balance of flavors and textures.

ingredients.notes: about 10-12 stalks, cut into 3-4 inch lengths

instructions.title: Prepare the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, toasted sesame seeds, minced garlic, and optional red chili flakes. Mix well and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Thoroughly clean and prepare your seafood. Peel and devein shrimp, then chop into bite-sized pieces. Clean squid and slice into rings. Cut green onions into 3-4 inch lengths. Thinly slice the yellow onion and optional red chili pepper.

instructions.title: Make the Pancake Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together all-purpose flour, rice flour, cornstarch, salt, the large egg, and cold water until just combined. Do not overmix; a few small lumps are acceptable. The batter should be a pourable consistency.

instructions.title: Assemble the Pancake
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Pour about 1/3 to 1/2 of the batter into the hot pan, spreading it thinly to form a round pancake. Immediately arrange a generous amount of green onions, sliced yellow onion, shrimp, squid, and a few slices of red chili pepper evenly over the batter.

instructions.title: Cook Until Golden and Crispy
instructions.text: Drizzle a little more oil around the edges of the pancake. Cook for 5-7 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy, and the edges begin to curl. Gently flip the pancake using a large spatula. Press down lightly to ensure all ingredients make contact with the pan.

instructions.title: Finish Cooking and Serve
instructions.text: Cook the second side for another 5-7 minutes until golden brown and the seafood is cooked through. If making multiple pancakes, add more oil to the pan as needed for each. Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board, slice into wedges, and serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce.

tips: For extra crispiness, use a very hot pan and a combination of all-purpose flour and rice flour in your batter. A little cornstarch also helps.
Do not overcrowd the pan with too much seafood or vegetables; this can make the pancake soggy. Cook in batches if necessary.
To prevent the pancake from sticking, ensure your pan is well-oiled and hot before adding the batter. A cast iron skillet works wonderfully for achieving a crispy crust.
Experiment with different seafood like mussels or oysters, or add other vegetables such as carrots or bell peppers for variety.
Serve immediately while hot and crispy. Reheating can diminish the crispiness, but a quick pan-fry can revive it.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to Korea and discover the irresistible charm of Haemul Pajeon, the beloved Korean seafood pancake. This savory dish is a staple in Korean cuisine, cherished for its crispy edges, tender interior, and a delightful medley of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. Whether you're a seasoned fan of Korean food or new to its incredible flavors, Haemul Pajeon offers a delicious and satisfying experience that perfectly balances texture and taste. It's more than just a pancake; it's a celebration of fresh ingredients, often enjoyed as a comforting snack, a hearty appetizer, or alongside a refreshing glass of makgeolli (Korean rice wine). Get ready to learn everything you need to know about this iconic dish, from its origins and essential ingredients to a step-by-step guide for making it perfectly at home.

## What is Haemul Pajeon?

Haemul Pajeon is a popular variety of *jeon*, a broad category of Korean pan-fried dishes. The name itself offers a clear description: "Haemul" means seafood, and "Pajeon" refers to a green onion pancake. Therefore, Haemul Pajeon is, quite simply, a seafood and green onion pancake. Unlike thicker, fluffier Western pancakes, Pajeon is typically thinner, with a focus on achieving a crispy, golden-brown crust while maintaining a soft, savory interior packed with flavorful ingredients.

Its origins are rooted in Korean culinary traditions, where *jeon* have been enjoyed for centuries, often served during holidays, celebrations, or as a comforting everyday meal. Haemul Pajeon stands out due to its generous inclusion of various seafood, which can range from succulent shrimp and tender squid to mussels, oysters, and clams, all harmoniously blended with an abundance of fresh green onions. The batter is light, allowing the natural flavors of the seafood and green onions to shine through, creating a truly unique and highly addictive savory treat. It’s a versatile dish, perfect for sharing and a fantastic introduction to the diverse world of Korean flavors.

## Ingredients for Authentic Haemul Pajeon

Crafting an authentic Haemul Pajeon begins with selecting fresh, high-quality ingredients. The beauty of this dish lies in its simplicity, allowing the core components to speak for themselves.

*   **For the Seafood:** A mix is highly recommended for varied textures and flavors. Popular choices include:
    *   Shrimp (peeled, deveined, and chopped into bite-sized pieces)
    *   Squid (cleaned and cut into rings or bite-sized pieces)
    *   Mussels or Clams (steamed and shelled, or fresh if preferred)
    *   Baby octopus or oysters (optional, for added variety)
    *   *Tip: If using frozen seafood, thaw thoroughly and pat dry to prevent a watery batter.*
*   **For the Vegetables:**
    *   **Green Onions (Scallions):** This is the star! You'll need a generous amount, cut into 2-3 inch lengths.
    *   Onion: Thinly sliced, adds a subtle sweetness.
    *   Carrot: Julienne-cut, for color and a slight crunch (optional).
    *   Red and Green Chili Peppers: Thinly sliced, for a mild kick and vibrant color (optional).
*   **For the Batter:**
    *   All-purpose flour: The base for the pancake.
    *   Glutinous rice flour or cornstarch: A small amount added for extra crispiness.
    *   Egg: Helps bind the batter and adds richness.
    *   Cold water or anchovy broth: Cold liquid helps achieve a crispier texture.
    *   Salt and white pepper: To season the batter.
*   **For Frying:**
    *   Vegetable oil or neutral oil: You'll need a good amount for pan-frying.

## How to Make Haemul Pajeon: Step-by-Step Guide

Making Haemul Pajeon at home is surprisingly straightforward, yielding incredibly satisfying results. Follow these steps for a perfect pancake every time:

1.  **Prepare the Seafood:** Thoroughly clean all your seafood. Pat shrimp and squid very dry with paper towels, then chop them into uniform, bite-sized pieces. If using mussels or clams, ensure they are cooked and shelled.
2.  **Prepare the Vegetables:** Wash and trim the green onions, cutting them into 2-3 inch pieces. Thinly slice the onion and julienne the carrot (if using). If adding chili peppers, slice them thinly.
3.  **Make the Batter:** In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, glutinous rice flour (or cornstarch), salt, and white pepper. In a separate bowl, whisk the egg with the cold water or anchovy broth. Gradually add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients, whisking until just combined. A few lumps are perfectly fine; overmixing can lead to a tough pancake. The batter should be thick enough to coat a spoon but still pourable.
4.  **Combine Ingredients:** Gently fold the prepared seafood, green onions, sliced onion, and any other vegetables into the batter. Ensure everything is evenly coated.
5.  **Heat the Pan:** Heat a large, non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add a generous amount of vegetable oil (about 2-3 tablespoons) to coat the bottom of the pan. The oil should be shimmering hot.
6.  **Cook the Pancake:** Pour about 1 to 1.5 cups of the batter mixture into the hot pan, spreading it evenly with the back of a ladle to form a round pancake, about 1/2 inch thick. If desired, you can artfully arrange a few extra pieces of green onion or seafood on top for presentation.
7.  **Fry until Golden:** Cook for 5-7 minutes on the first side, or until the edges are golden brown and crispy, and the bottom is cooked through. You can gently lift an edge with a spatula to check.
8.  **Flip and Finish:** Carefully flip the pancake using a large spatula (or two spatulas for easier handling). Add a little more oil around the edges if the pan looks dry. Cook for another 5-7 minutes on the second side until golden brown and cooked through.
9.  **Serve:** Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board and cut into wedges or squares. Serve immediately with the dipping sauce. Repeat the process for the remaining batter, adding more oil as needed for each pancake.

## Pro Tips for the Crispiest Haemul Pajeon

Achieving that coveted crispy exterior and tender, flavorful interior for your Haemul Pajeon is all about technique. Here are some pro tips to elevate your pancake game:

*   **Use Cold Liquid in the Batter:** This is a game-changer! Cold water or broth helps inhibit gluten development, resulting in a lighter, crispier texture when fried.
*   **Don't Overmix the Batter:** A slightly lumpy batter is actually desirable. Overmixing develops gluten, which can make your pancake tough instead of tender and crispy.
*   **The Right Flour Blend:** While all-purpose flour is the base, incorporating a small amount of glutinous rice flour or cornstarch significantly enhances crispiness. Some Korean pancake mixes (Buchim Garu) already contain this blend.
*   **Plenty of Hot Oil:** Don't be shy with the oil. Haemul Pajeon is pan-fried, not just sautéed. You need enough oil to create that beautiful, crispy crust. Ensure the oil is shimmering hot before adding the batter.
*   **Even Heat Distribution:** Use a well-seasoned cast-iron skillet or a heavy-bottomed non-stick pan that distributes heat evenly.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Pan:** Cook one pancake at a time, or no more than two if your pan is very large. Overcrowding lowers the pan's temperature and steams the pancake instead of frying it.
*   **Press Gently:** After flipping, gently press down on the pancake with your spatula. This ensures maximum contact with the hot pan and oil, promoting even crisping.
*   **Cook Until Deep Golden:** Resist the urge to remove the pancake too early. A deep golden-brown color indicates optimal crispiness and flavor development.

## Haemul Pajeon Dipping Sauce Recipe

No Haemul Pajeon is complete without a zesty, savory dipping sauce to complement its rich flavors. This simple yet essential sauce adds a burst of umami and a hint of tang.

**Ingredients:**

*   3 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   1/2 teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes) (optional, for a kick)
*   1 teaspoon minced garlic (optional)
*   1 teaspoon finely chopped green onion (optional, for freshness)
*   A pinch of sugar (optional, to balance the flavors)

**Instructions:**

1.  In a small bowl, combine the soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil.
2.  Add the toasted sesame seeds.
3.  If desired, stir in the gochugaru, minced garlic, chopped green onion, and a pinch of sugar.
4.  Whisk all ingredients together until well combined.
5.  Taste and adjust seasonings as needed. You might prefer more vinegar for tang or more sugar for sweetness.

This dipping sauce is incredibly versatile and can be used for various Korean pancakes and dumplings.

## Serving Suggestions & Variations

Haemul Pajeon is incredibly versatile, making it suitable for a variety of dining occasions.

*   **Traditional Pairing:** In Korea, Haemul Pajeon is famously paired with *makgeolli*, a lightly sparkling, milky Korean rice wine. The crispness of the pancake and the refreshing, slightly sweet and tangy notes of makgeolli create a harmonious balance, especially on a rainy day. It also pairs wonderfully with *soju*.
*   **Appetizer or Side Dish:** Serve Haemul Pajeon as a fantastic appetizer before a larger Korean meal, or as a flavorful side dish alongside other Korean BBQ or stews.
*   **Light Meal:** It can easily stand alone as a satisfying light lunch or dinner, especially when accompanied by a fresh salad or a bowl of rice.
*   **Variations to Try:**
    *   **Kimchi Pajeon:** For a spicy and tangy twist, add chopped kimchi to your batter. Squeeze out excess liquid from the kimchi first.
    *   **Vegetarian Pajeon:** Omit the seafood and load up on other vegetables like mushrooms (shiitake, oyster), zucchini, bell peppers, or even sweet potato.
    *   **Different Seafood Combinations:** Feel free to experiment with your favorite seafood. Scallops, small pieces of firm white fish, or a mixture of all the above can create exciting new flavors.
    *   **Meat Pajeon (Yukjeon):** While not Haemul Pajeon, you can make *Yukjeon* by thinly slicing beef, dipping it in batter, and pan-frying.

## Frequently Asked Questions About Haemul Pajeon

**Q: Can I make Haemul Pajeon ahead of time?**
A: Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed fresh, straight from the pan, to fully appreciate its crispy texture. However, you can prepare the seafood and chop the green onions ahead of time. Keep the batter separate and mix everything just before cooking.

**Q: How do I reheat leftover Haemul Pajeon?**
A: To restore its crispiness, reheat Haemul Pajeon in a dry skillet over medium heat or in an air fryer until warmed through and the edges are crisp again. Avoid microwaving, as it will make the pancake soggy.

**Q: What kind of seafood is best for Haemul Pajeon?**
A: A mix of seafood provides the best texture and flavor. Shrimp and squid are staples. Mussels, oysters, and small pieces of fish can also be excellent additions. Always opt for fresh seafood if possible, or high-quality frozen and properly thawed.

**Q: Is Haemul Pajeon gluten-free?**
A: Traditional Haemul Pajeon is made with wheat flour, so it is not gluten-free. However, you can substitute the all-purpose flour with a gluten-free flour blend (often containing rice flour, tapioca starch, and potato starch) for a gluten-free version. Ensure your chosen blend works well for pan-frying.

**Q: What's the difference between Pajeon and Jeon?**
A: *Jeon* is a broad term in Korean cuisine for any dish made by pan-frying ingredients coated in an egg-and-flour batter. Think of it as the Korean equivalent of a fritter or pancake. *Pajeon* is a specific type of *jeon* that primarily features green onions (*pa* meaning green onion) as its main ingredient. Haemul Pajeon simply specifies that seafood is also included.

Haemul Pajeon is more than just a dish; it's an experience that brings the vibrant flavors of Korea right into your kitchen. With its irresistible combination of fresh seafood, aromatic green onions, and a perfectly crispy exterior, it's a culinary delight that appeals to everyone. Don't hesitate to try making this delicious Korean seafood pancake yourself – you'll be rewarded with a truly authentic taste of Korea that's perfect for any occasion.
